segments.text: Come, and let us return to the Lord; For He has torn, but He will heal us; He has stricken, but He will bind us up.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: After two days He will revive us; On the third day He will raise us up, That we may live in His sight.
Let us know, Let us pursue the knowledge of the Lord.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: His going forth is established as the morning; He will come to us like the rain, Like the latter and former rain to the earth.
O Ephraim, what shall I do to you? O Judah, what shall I do to you? For your faithfulness is like a morning cloud, And like the early dew it goes away.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Therefore I have hewn them by the prophets, I have slain them by the words of My mouth; And your judgments are like light that goes forth.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For I desire mercy and not sacrifice, And the knowledge of God more than burnt offerings.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But like men they transgressed the covenant; There they dealt treacherously with Me.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Gilead is a city of evildoers And defiled with blood.
As bands of robbers lie in wait for a man, So the company of priests murder on the way to Shechem; Surely they commit lewdness.
I have seen a horrible thing in the house of Israel: There is the harlotry of Ephraim; Israel is defiled.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Also, O Judah, a harvest is appointed for you, When I return the captives of My people.
segments.speaker: God
